behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification quality, not just local routing logic. A stubbed classifier would always return the expected label and could hide misses on explicit imagery. No multi-agent sequence is required beyond one model decision plus deterministic hold logic, so Agentic (not Workflow) is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit images to the real vision moderation model through the moderation service and assert each submission is held-for-review and not published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real model robustness to obfuscation and unicode variants. Deterministic mocks cannot validate whether the model actually catches these transformed inputs. It is a single model-quality decision, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Send a suite of obfuscated hate-speech text samples (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing tricks) through the real text moderation model and verify they are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily cross-module handoff/orchestration: two check results must propagate into a publish decision and persistence path. The behavior text does not require validating model-output quality; pass/fail outputs can be controlled with deterministic fakes. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness here is decision wiring after checks, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service + decision engine + publish/store modules with stubbed image/text check clients returning pass; assert the post is published exactly once and not held/dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly spans multiple model-involved stages in sequence and asserts end-to-end latency of that chain. A lower-level test can measure isolated logic but may miss cross-step timing interactions and queueing across agentic calls. Rejected lower levels: Integration/System alone are insufficient because the requirement is the full multi-step moderation flow including sequential AI checks.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through real image and text moderation calls plus final decision path, measure submission-to-terminal-decision latency, and assert p95 <= 5s under representative peak-load traffic.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is module interaction between moderation logic and retry queue state transitions on dependency failure. This can be caught with a deterministic unavailable-response/error from the vision client and real queue/repository wiring in-process. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real network outage semantics; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model-output-content quality is being asserted.
behaviors.test_description: With moderation service and retry queue modules wired, force vision-client failure (e.g., unavailable error/503), submit image posts, and assert they enter retry queue and are never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This directly targets consistency of real model decisions on borderline content, where nondeterminism/model variance is the failure source. A mock would be artificially consistent and hide real drift/flapping. Rejected Workflow: repeated submissions are multiple trials, not a required multi-agent decision chain within one workflow execution.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined short window through the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency meets the product threshold (e.g., no flag/publish flapping beyond allowed tolerance).
